description: Evidence-first performance profiling — tool selection, methodology, interpretation. Use BEFORE guessing at performance issues. Tools first, logs second, code last. Covers: slow queries, high latency, CPU spikes, memory growth, throughput regressions (including 'got slower after deploy' — measure before assuming the deploy diff is the cause). Not for: crashes / logic errors (→ debug), tests being slow as test design issue (→ test-strategy).

results.query: the API got slower after we deployed yesterday
the /api/dashboard endpoint p99 latency went from 300ms to 2.5s over the last week. need to figure out where the time is going.
the gobe Rust service has been creeping up to 8GB RAM in production. it was steady at 2GB a month ago. profile what's leaking.
我們的 query 在 staging 跑得超慢但 dev 機正常。需要找出 bottleneck — DB? code? network?
the CPU is pegged at 100% during peak hours. which functions are eating it? need a flame graph or similar.
before we approve this PR, I want to make sure it didn't regress baseline performance — can we measure before/after?
the payment handler is crashing with SIGSEGV on certain inputs. find the bug.
implement caching for the dashboard query — it's slow because we hit DB on every request
what's the performance characteristic of HashMap vs BTreeMap in Rust for our use case? research before deciding
save the lesson we just learned about Postgres autovacuum being the cause of the latency spikes
let's plan the migration from gunicorn to uvicorn. impacts: deploy, monitoring, latency profile
